title: taraxacum consobrinum
intro: Taraxacum consobrinum, also known as the lesser dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, belonging to the sunflower family. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum consobrinum lie in its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. The plant is used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. Taraxacum consobrinum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be consumed as a raw vegetable or used in traditional herbal remedies.
